header.title: Protect PDF — Add Password to PDF Files Online Free
header.description: Free online PDF protection tool to add password security to your documents. Encrypt PDFs with user passwords and set owner permissions for printing, copying, or editing. Secure sensitive files instantly — 100% client-side, nothing uploaded.
header.keywords: protect pdf, add password to pdf, pdf encryption, password protect pdf, pdf locker, secure pdf, lock pdf file, encrypt pdf online

privacy.notice: 100% client-side. Your PDF is encrypted entirely in the browser — nothing is uploaded to any server.

permissions.title: Permissions
permissions.whatUsersCanDo: What users can do after opening

passwordReminder.title: Remember your password.
passwordReminder.description: There is no way to recover a forgotten PDF password. Save it in a password manager.

howItWorks.title: How PDF Protection Works
howItWorks.userPasswordDesc: required to open the PDF. Key derived via MD5 (50 rounds) then RC4-128 encryption is applied to all content.
howItWorks.ownerPasswordDesc: separate admin password that can bypass permission restrictions.
howItWorks.permissionsDesc: restrict printing, copying, editing, and annotations even after the PDF is opened.
howItWorks.compatibilityDesc: RC4-128 (PDF 1.4) is the most widely supported encryption format, accepted by every PDF viewer.

encryption.title: RC4-128 Encryption
encryption.description: PDF Standard Security Handler r3. Works in every PDF viewer: Chrome, Firefox, Edge, Safari, Acrobat, Preview.

howToUse.title: How to Use Protect PDF
howToUse.step1Desc: Drop or upload the PDF file you want to password-protect.
howToUse.step2Desc: Enter a user password (required to open) and optionally an owner password (admin access).
howToUse.step3Desc: Set what users can do after opening: printing, copying, modifying, annotating, and form filling.
howToUse.step4Desc: Click 'Protect PDF' and download your encrypted file — all encryption happens locally in your browser.

items.question: What encryption algorithm is used?
items.answer: PDF Standard Security Handler r3 with RC4-128 encryption. This is the most widely compatible format supported by every major PDF viewer.

items.question: What is the difference between user and owner password?
items.answer: The user password is required to open the PDF. The owner password is an optional admin password that bypasses permission restrictions.

items.question: Can I recover a forgotten password?
items.answer: No. There is no way to recover a forgotten PDF password. Always save passwords in a password manager.

items.question: What permissions can I restrict?
items.answer: Printing (low or high quality), copying text and images, modifying content, adding annotations, filling forms, and assembling pages.

items.question: Will the protected PDF open in any viewer?
items.answer: Yes. RC4-128 encryption is supported in Chrome, Firefox, Edge, Safari, Adobe Acrobat, and Apple Preview.

items.question: Is my PDF uploaded during encryption?
items.answer: No. All encryption is performed locally in your browser using pdf-lib. Your file never leaves your device.

items.question: Can I remove protection later?
items.answer: Yes. Use the Unlock PDF tool if you know the password. Always keep a backup of the unprotected original.

examples.title: Try an Example
examples.stats: 1 file encrypted with user password and restricted printing
